### Information

Packages required for these analyses: ape, corrr, devtools, dplyr, empire, geojsonsf, ggplot2, ggtree, grid, gridExtra, HDInterval, httr2, knitr, lubridate, mcmcse, patchwork, phytools, raster, rgplates, sf, sfheaders, spatstat, spind, stringr, treeio

```{r setup, echo=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval=FALSE,echo=FALSE,results=FALSE,message=FALSE,warning=FALSE,tidy='styler')
RERUN <- FALSE
```


```{r plot_settings, eval=TRUE}
plot_themes <- c("dark","light","plain","poster","manuscript","manuscript2","manuscript3","manuscript4")
set_plot_theme <- function(plot_theme) {
  if (plot_theme == "dark") {
    lines_color <<- "white"
    background_color <<- "black"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray24"
    range_color <<- "cyan"
    ellipse_color <<- "coral"
    color_1 <<- "cyan"
    color_2 <<- "coral"
    color_3 <<- "springgreen"
    color_4 <<- "mediumpurple"
    abline_color <<- "mediumpurple"
    vline_color <<- "red"
  }
  if (plot_theme == "light") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "cyan3"
    ellipse_color <<- "coral"
    color_1 <<- "cyan3"
    color_2 <<- "coral"
    color_3 <<- "springgreen3"
    color_4 <<- "mediumpurple"
    abline_color <<- "mediumpurple"
    vline_color <<- "red"
  }
  if (plot_theme == "plain") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "gray24"
    ellipse_color <<- "black"
    color_1 <<- "gray"
    color_2 <<- "gray"
    color_3 <<- "gray"
    color_4 <<- "gray"
    abline_color <<- "black"
    vline_color <<- "gray"
  }
  if (plot_theme == "poster") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "gray24"
    ellipse_color <<- "#a51417ff"
    color_1 <<- "gray"
    color_2 <<- "gray"
    color_3 <<- "gray"
    color_4 <<- "gray"
    abline_color <<- "#a51417ff"
    vline_color <<- "#a51417ff"
  }
  # THEME FOR SINGLE-LINEAGE MAP IN THE CONCEPTUAL FIGURE
  if (plot_theme == "manuscript") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "gray"
    ellipse_color <<- "black"
    color_1 <<- "gray60"
    color_2 <<- "lightgray"
    color_3 <<- "black"
    color_4 <<- "black"
    abline_color <<- "black"
    vline_color <<- "gray"
  }
  # THEME FOR SIMULATION RESULTS
  if (plot_theme == "manuscript2") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "gray"
    ellipse_color <<- "black"
    color_1 <<- "gray"
    color_2 <<- "gray24"
    color_3 <<- "black"
    color_4 <<- "black"
    abline_color <<- "black"
    vline_color <<- "gray"
  }
  # THEME FOR ANCESTRAL STATES MAP
  if (plot_theme == "manuscript3") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "gray"
    ellipse_color <<- "black"
    color_1 <<- "gray"
    color_2 <<- "gray"
    color_3 <<- "gray"
    color_4 <<- "gray"
    abline_color <<- "black"
    vline_color <<- "gray"
  }
  # THEME FOR ARIDITY FIGURE
  if (plot_theme == "manuscript4") {
    lines_color <<- "black"
    background_color <<- "white"
    shape_color <<- "gray24"
    shape_color_2 <<- "gray"
    range_color <<- "gray"
    ellipse_color <<- "black"
    color_1 <<- "black"
    color_2 <<- "gray"
    color_3 <<- "lightgray"
    color_4 <<- "black"
    abline_color <<- "black"
    vline_color <<- "gray"
  }
}
set_plot_theme("dark")
```

```{r tree_data, eval=TRUE}
tree <- ape::read.tree(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/spheno.tre"))
tree$tip.label <- gsub("_"," ",tree$tip.label)
empire_tree <- make_detailedTree(tree)
```

```{r range_polygons}
# SUBSETTING SHAPEFILE -- DO NOT NEED TO RERUN
if (RERUN) {
  shapefile <- sf::read_sf(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/ranges/Gard_1_7_ranges.shp"))
  taxa_list <- tree$tip.label
  spheno_shapes <- shapefile[shapefile$binomial %in% taxa_list,]
  st_write(spheno_shapes,paste0(local_directory,"skinks/ranges/australian_skinks.shp"))
  sf_use_s2(FALSE)
  australia_shape_all <- st_union(spheno_shapes)
  australia_shape_all <- sf_remove_holes(australia_shape_all)
  australia_shape_all <- st_cast(australia_shape_all, "POLYGON")
  australia_shape <- australia_shape_all[4]
  sf_use_s2(TRUE)
  coords <- st_coordinates(australia_shape)
  poly1 <- sp::Polygon(cbind(coords[,1],coords[,2]))
  firstPoly <- sp::Polygons(list(poly1),ID="Australia")
  firstSpatialPoly <- sp::SpatialPolygons(list(firstPoly))
  shapefile(firstSpatialPoly,file=paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/australia/australia.shp"))
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

```{r make_ellipses}
# MAKING ELLIPSES -- DO NOT NEED TO RERUN
if (RERUN) {
  set.seed(16)
  data <- get_ellipses(spheno_shapes)
  write.csv(data,paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/ranges/ellipse_data.csv"),row.names=FALSE,quote=FALSE)
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

```{r range_data, eval=TRUE}
spheno_shapes <- sf::read_sf(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/ranges/australian_skinks.shp"))
australia_shape <- sf::read_sf(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/australia/australia.shp"))
australia_shape <- st_set_crs(australia_shape,4326)
ellipse_data <- read.csv(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/ranges/ellipse_data.csv"),header=TRUE)
max_area <- exp(get_ellipses(australia_shape)$a) # max 'a' ~6.49, max area ~652 square degrees
elongation_x <- st_bbox(australia_shape)$xmax - st_bbox(australia_shape)$xmin
elongation_y <- st_bbox(australia_shape)$ymax - st_bbox(australia_shape)$ymin
elongation_percent <- (elongation_x - elongation_y) / elongation_x # difference of ~30%
```

## Analytics

<div class = "row">

```{r completed_analyses}
# CHECKING FOR COMPLETED ANALYSES AND RUNTIMES -- DO NOT RERUN
if (RERUN) {
  run_log <- read.csv(paste0(cluster_directory,"joblogs/run_log.txt"),sep="\t",header=FALSE)
  completed_rows <- 200000
  completed_rows_rase <- 10000
  
  run_data <- data.frame(matrix(NA,nrow=0,ncol=3))
  for (i in 1:200) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("full","sim",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  for (i in 1:100) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("full","sensitivity",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  for (i in 1:100) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("full","noisy",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  for (i in 1:100) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("full","rase",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  for (i in 1:10) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("standard","skinks",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  for (i in 1:5) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("shuffled","skinks",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  for (i in 1:5) {run_data <- rbind(run_data,c("under_prior","skinks",stringr::str_pad(i, 3, pad = "0")))}
  colnames(run_data) <- c("condition","dataset","number")
  run_data$ended <- FALSE
  run_data$n_iter <- NA
  run_data$completed <- FALSE
  run_data$tree_size <- NA
  run_data$hours <- NA
  
  for (i in 1:nrow(run_log)) {
    name <- run_log[i,1]
    analysis_type <- unlist(strsplit(name,".",fixed=TRUE))
    row <- which(run_data$condition == analysis_type[1] & run_data$dataset == analysis_type[2] & run_data$number == stringr::str_pad(analysis_type[3], 3, pad = "0"))
    run_data$ended[row] <- TRUE
    end_times <- unlist(strsplit(run_log[i,3],"_",fixed=TRUE))
    end_time <- as.POSIXct(paste(end_times[1],end_times[2],sep=" "))
    start_times <- unlist(strsplit(run_log[i,2],"_",fixed=TRUE))
    start_time <- as.POSIXct(paste(start_times[1],start_times[2],sep=" "))
    duration_hours <- round(difftime(end_time,start_time,units="hours"),3)
    run_data$hours[row] <- duration_hours
  }
  
  nrows <- nrow(run_data)
  for (i in 1:nrow(run_data)) {
    cat("\r","Row ", i, " | ", round(as.numeric(i)/nrows*100,0), "%")
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "full" & run_data$dataset[i] == "sim") {
      directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,run_data$number[i],"/")
      treefile <- ape::read.tree(paste0(directory,"true.tree.txt"))
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(treefile$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations * 10
        if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "full" & run_data$dataset[i] == "sensitivity") {
      directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,run_data$number[i],"/sensitivity/")
        treefile <- ape::read.tree(paste0(directory,"true.tree.txt"))
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(treefile$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations * 10
        if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "full" & run_data$dataset[i] == "noisy") {
      directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,run_data$number[i],"/noisy/")
      treefile <- ape::read.tree(paste0(directory,"true.tree.txt"))
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(treefile$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations * 10
        if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "full" & run_data$dataset[i] == "rase") {
      directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,run_data$number[i],"/")
      treefile <- ape::read.tree(paste0(directory,"true.tree.txt"))
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(treefile$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"rase.csv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(unlist(strsplit(system(paste0("wc -l ",directory,"rase.csv"),intern=TRUE),"/V")[[1]][1])) - 1
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations
        if (iterations >= completed_rows_rase) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "standard" & run_data$dataset[i] == "skinks") {
      directory <- paste0(cluster_directory,"skinks/output/standard.skinks.",run_data$number[i],"/")
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(ape::read.tree(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/spheno.tre"))$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations * 10
        if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "shuffled" & run_data$dataset[i] == "skinks") {
      directory <- paste0(cluster_directory,"skinks/output/shuffled.skinks.",run_data$number[i],"/")
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(ape::read.tree(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/spheno.tre"))$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations * 10
        if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
    if (run_data$condition[i] == "under_prior" & run_data$dataset[i] == "skinks") {
      directory <- paste0(cluster_directory,"skinks/output/under_prior.skinks.",run_data$number[i],"/")
      run_data$tree_size[i] <- length(ape::read.tree(paste0(local_directory,"skinks/data/spheno.tre"))$tip.label)
      if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
        run_data$n_iter[i] <- iterations * 10
        if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {run_data$completed[i] <- TRUE}
      }
    }
  }
  
  write.table(run_data,file=paste0(cluster_directory,"joblogs/run_data.tsv"),quote=FALSE,sep="\t",row.names=FALSE)
  
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

```{r analytics}
run_data <- read.csv(paste0(cluster_directory,"joblogs/run_data.tsv"),sep="\t")

data_summary <- data.frame(matrix(NA,nrow=6,ncol=5))
colnames(data_summary) <- c("Type", "Ended", "Completed", "Size", "Hours")
data_summary$Type <- c("Full Sim", "Full Sensitivity", "Full Noisy", "Full Rase", "Standard Skinks", "Shuffled Skinks")

data_summary$Ended[1] <- sum(run_data$ended[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sim")])
data_summary$Ended[2] <- sum(run_data$ended[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sensitivity")])
data_summary$Ended[3] <- sum(run_data$ended[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "noisy")])
data_summary$Ended[4] <- sum(run_data$ended[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "rase")])
data_summary$Ended[5] <- sum(run_data$ended[which(run_data$condition == "standard" & run_data$dataset == "skinks")])
data_summary$Ended[6] <- sum(run_data$ended[which(run_data$condition == "shuffled" & run_data$dataset == "skinks")])

data_summary$Completed[1] <- sum(run_data$completed[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sim")])
data_summary$Completed[2] <- sum(run_data$completed[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sensitivity")])
data_summary$Completed[3] <- sum(run_data$completed[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "noisy")])
data_summary$Completed[4] <- sum(run_data$completed[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "rase")])
data_summary$Completed[5] <- sum(run_data$completed[which(run_data$condition == "standard" & run_data$dataset == "skinks")])
data_summary$Completed[6] <- sum(run_data$completed[which(run_data$condition == "shuffled" & run_data$dataset == "skinks")])

data_summary$Size[1] <- round(mean(run_data$tree_size[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sim")]))
data_summary$Size[2] <- round(mean(run_data$tree_size[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sensitivity")]))
data_summary$Size[3] <- round(mean(run_data$tree_size[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "noisy")]))
data_summary$Size[4] <- round(mean(run_data$tree_size[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "rase")]))
data_summary$Size[5] <- round(mean(run_data$tree_size[which(run_data$condition == "standard" & run_data$dataset == "skinks")]))
data_summary$Size[6] <- round(mean(run_data$tree_size[which(run_data$condition == "shuffled" & run_data$dataset == "skinks")]))

data_summary$Hours[1] <- round(mean(run_data$hours[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sim" & run_data$hours > .1)]))
data_summary$Hours[2] <- round(mean(run_data$hours[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "sensitivity" & run_data$hours > .1)]))
data_summary$Hours[3] <- round(mean(run_data$hours[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "noisy" & run_data$hours > .1)]))
data_summary$Hours[4] <- round(mean(run_data$hours[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "rase" & run_data$hours > .1)]))
data_summary$Hours[5] <- round(mean(run_data$hours[which(run_data$condition == "standard" & run_data$dataset == "skinks" & run_data$hours > .1)]))
data_summary$Hours[6] <- round(mean(run_data$hours[which(run_data$condition == "shuffled" & run_data$dataset == "skinks" & run_data$hours > .1)]))

data_summary_fancy <- data_summary
colnames(data_summary_fancy) <- c("Analysis Type", "Number\nEnded", "Number\nCompleted", "Mean Tree\nSize (taxa)", "Mean\nDuration (hr)")

theme <- ttheme_minimal(base_size=14, 
                        core=list(bg_params=list(fill=background_color,col=background_color),
                                  fg_params=list(col=lines_color)),
                        colhead=list(bg_params=list(fill=shape_color,col=lines_color),
                                     fg_params=list(col=lines_color,fontface=2)))
g <- tableGrob(data_summary_fancy,rows=NULL,theme=theme)
widthsIn <- lapply(g$widths, function(x) {convertUnit(x, unitTo = "inch", valueOnly = TRUE)})
heigthsIn <- lapply(g$heights, function(x) {convertUnit(x, unitTo = "inch", valueOnly = TRUE)})
w <- sum(unlist(widthsIn))
h <- sum(unlist(heigthsIn))

png(paste0(other_plot_directory,"data_summary.png"),height=h,width=w,units="in",res=400)
grid.table(data_summary_fancy,rows=NULL,theme=theme)
dev.off()

print(run_data)
```

```{r analytics_show, eval=TRUE, results=TRUE}
include_graphics(paste0(other_plot_directory,"data_summary.png"))
```

---

Complete list of analyses:

* 200 full simulation analyses
* 100 analyses for sensitivity to missing taxa
* 100 analyses for sensitivity to noise
* 100 *rase* analyses for comparison
* 10 empirical analysis for skinks
* 5 skink analyses with shuffled tips

</div>
<div class = "col-md-6">

Here are the run times for the simulation analyses based on the sizes of the phylogenetic trees. Note that there is a logarithmic relationship. We did not increase the number of data augmentation proposals with tree size.

```{r times, echo=TRUE}
run_data <- read.csv(paste0(cluster_directory,"joblogs/run_data.tsv"),sep="\t")
run_data_subset <- run_data[which(run_data$condition == "full" & run_data$dataset == "noisy" & run_data$completed == TRUE),]
coefs <- coef(lm(hours~log(tree_size),data=run_data_subset))
times_plot <- ggplot(run_data_subset,aes(x=tree_size,y=hours)) +
  geom_point(color=lines_color) +
  stat_smooth(method="lm",formula=y~log(x),color=abline_color) +
  scale_x_continuous(limits=c(20,240),breaks=seq(20,240,20),labels=seq(20,240,20)) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits=c(25,275),breaks=seq(50,250,50)) +
  labs(x="Tree Size (number of taxa)",y="Run Time (hours)",title=paste0("hours = ",round(coefs[2])," x ln(# of taxa) - ",round(abs(coefs[1])))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=lines_color),
        plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
        panel.grid.major=element_line(color=shape_color),
        panel.grid.minor.y=element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor.x=element_blank(),
        title=element_text(color=lines_color),
        axis.title.x=element_text(color=lines_color,size=16),
        axis.title.y=element_text(color=lines_color,angle=90,size=16),
        axis.text.x=element_text(color=lines_color),
        axis.text.y=element_text(color=lines_color))
ggsave(paste0(other_plot_directory,"times.png"),times_plot,dpi=600,width=8,height=6)
ggsave(paste0(other_plot_directory,"times_2.png"),times_plot,dpi=600,width=9,height=4)
```

```{r times_show, eval=TRUE, results=TRUE}
include_graphics(paste0(other_plot_directory,"times.png"))
```

</div>
</div>

## Model

### Example Ellipses

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-7">

Here is an example plot of an ellipse at cladogenesis. The plotting function `plot_scenario_annotated()` includes all necessary information about the cladogenetic scenario, and plots what happens in "real" space.

There are two possible cladogenetic modes: budding and splitting. This example uses a splitting scenario, where the ancestral area is partitioned between the daughters, and the daughters are adjacent but not overlapping.

Inheritance under this scenario can be symmetric or asymmetric, determined by the value of $c$. In this case, inheritance is asymmetric.

```{r example_scenario, echo=TRUE}
x <- 0
y <- 0
r <- 3
s <- .5
a <- 3
d <- 1
m <- 1
c <- .5
hind <- 2
hvals <- c(0,pi/4,pi/2,3*pi/4,pi,5*pi/4,3*pi/2,7*pi/4)
h <- hvals[hind]
z <- 5
alpha <- -3

scenario_plot <- plot_scenario_annotated(x,y,r,s,a,d,m,c,h,z,alpha)
ggsave(paste0(other_plot_directory,"example_scenario.png"),scenario_plot,dpi=600,width=7,height=5.5)
```

</div>
<div class = "col-md-5">

```{r example_scenario_show, eval=TRUE, results=TRUE}
include_graphics(paste0(other_plot_directory,"example_scenario.png"))
```

</div>
</div>

## Dataset

### Modern Ellipses

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-9">

Here are some example ranges and their corresponding ellipses. Shown: *Lerista fragilis* (northeast), *Ctenotus australis* (southwest), *Ctenotus militaris* (north), *Lerista xanthura* (central west), *Lerista edwardsae* (south), *Eulamprus tympanum* (southeast). The largest possible range, encompassing all of Australia, would have a maximum area of ~652 square degrees, or a log area ('a' value) of ~6.49. Most ranges are mid-sized, like those shown here.

```{r modern_ellipse_plot_without_ranges}
plot_without_ranges <- ggplot(spheno_shapes) +
  geom_sf(fill=lines_color,color=NA,alpha=0.04) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA))
ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"australia_no_ranges.png"),plot_without_ranges,dpi=600)
```

```{r modern_ellipse_plot_with_ranges}
# ELLIPSES TO INCLUDE
# 157: Lerista fragilis (northeast)
# 27: Ctenotus australis (southwest)
# 64: Ctenotus militaris (north)
# 208: Lerista xanthura (central west)
# 151: Lerista edwardsae (south)
# 116: Eulamprus tympanum (southeast)

plot_with_ranges <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
  geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[157,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[27,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[64,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[208,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[151,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[116,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA))
ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"australia_with_ranges.png"),plot_with_ranges,dpi=600)
```

```{r modern_ellipse_plot_with_ellipses, echo=TRUE}
ellipse_157 <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[157],ellipse_data$y[157],ellipse_data$r[157],ellipse_data$s[157],ellipse_data$a[157])
ellipse_27 <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[27],ellipse_data$y[27],ellipse_data$r[27],ellipse_data$s[27],ellipse_data$a[27])
ellipse_64 <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[64],ellipse_data$y[64],ellipse_data$r[64],ellipse_data$s[64],ellipse_data$a[64])
ellipse_208 <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[208],ellipse_data$y[208],ellipse_data$r[208],ellipse_data$s[208],ellipse_data$a[208])
ellipse_151 <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[151],ellipse_data$y[151],ellipse_data$r[151],ellipse_data$s[151],ellipse_data$a[151])
ellipse_116 <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[116],ellipse_data$y[116],ellipse_data$r[116],ellipse_data$s[116],ellipse_data$a[116])

plot_with_ellipses <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
  geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[157,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +

  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[27,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_polygon(data=ellipse_27,aes(x=x,y=y),fill=NA,color=ellipse_color,lwd=2) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[64,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
  geom_polygon(data=ellipse_64,aes(x=x,y=y),fill=NA,color=ellipse_color,lwd=2) +
  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[208,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +

  geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[151,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +

ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"australia_with_ellipses.png"),plot_with_ellipses,dpi=600)
```

```{r modern_ellipses_by_species}
# PLOTS FOR INDIVIDUAL SPECIES - DO NOT RERUN
if (RERUN) {
  n_species <- nrow(ellipse_data)
  for (i in 1:n_species) {
    species <- ellipse_data$taxon[i]
    cat("\r","Species: ", species, " | ", round(as.numeric(i)/n_species*100,0), "%                    ")
    ellipse <- make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data$x[i],ellipse_data$y[i],ellipse_data$r[i],ellipse_data$s[i],ellipse_data$a[i])
  plot_species <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
    geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
    geom_sf(data=spheno_shapes[i,],fill=range_color,color=range_color,alpha=0.6) +
    geom_polygon(data=ellipse,aes(x=x,y=y),fill=NA,color=ellipse_color,lwd=2) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA))
  ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"species/",i,"_",gsub(" ","_",species),".png"),plot_species,dpi=600)
  }
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

And here are all of the modern ellipses, colored by clade.

```{r modern_ellipses_all, echo=TRUE}
ellipse_data_grouped <- ellipse_data
for (i in 1:nrow(ellipse_data_grouped)) {
  taxon <- as.character(ellipse_data_grouped$taxon[i])
  clade <- "Third Clade"
  if (grepl("Ctenotus", taxon, fixed=TRUE)) {clade <- "Ctenotus"}
  if (grepl("Lerista", taxon, fixed=TRUE)) {clade <- "Lerista"}
  if (grepl("Notoscincus ornatus", taxon, fixed=TRUE)) {clade <- "X"}
  ellipse_data_grouped$Clade[i] <- clade
}

polygons_data <- data.frame(matrix(data=NA,nrow=0,ncol=4))
for (i in 1:nrow(ellipse_data_grouped)) {
  ellipse <- data.frame(make_ellipse_coords(ellipse_data_grouped$x[i],ellipse_data_grouped$y[i],ellipse_data_grouped$r[i],ellipse_data_grouped$s[i],ellipse_data_grouped$a[i]))
  n_points <- nrow(ellipse)
  ellipse$taxon <- rep(ellipse_data_grouped$taxon[i],n_points)
  ellipse$Clade <- rep(ellipse_data_grouped$Clade[i],n_points)
  polygons_data <- rbind(polygons_data,ellipse)
}
polygons_data$taxon <- as.factor(polygons_data$taxon)
polygons_data$Clade <- as.factor(polygons_data$Clade)

xmin <- min(min(ellipse_data_grouped$x),min(polygons_data$x))
xmax <- max(max(ellipse_data_grouped$x),max(polygons_data$x))
ymin <- min(min(ellipse_data_grouped$y),min(polygons_data$y))
ymax <- max(max(ellipse_data_grouped$y),max(polygons_data$y))

node_directions <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
  lims(x=c(xmin,xmax),y=c(ymin,ymax)) +
  geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
  geom_polygon(data=polygons_data,aes(x=x,y=y,group=taxon,color=Clade),fill=NA,lwd=.25) +
  #annotate(geom="text",x=ellipse_data_grouped$x,y=ellipse_data_grouped$y,label=ellipse_data_grouped$taxon,color=lines_color,size=1) +
  scale_color_manual(values=c(color_2,color_3,color_4,color_1), 
                     labels=c(bquote(italic(.("Ctenotus"))), bquote(italic(.("Lerista"))), "Third Clade", "Other"),
                     guide=guide_legend(override.aes=list(linetype=c(0,0,0,0),fill=c(color_2,color_3,color_4,color_1)))) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color=background_color,linewidth=2),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color),
        legend.position=c(.85,.8),
        legend.title=element_text(color=lines_color),
        legend.text=element_text(color=lines_color),
        legend.key.size=unit(.4,"cm"))

ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"ellipses_by_clade.png"),node_directions,dpi=600,width=7,height=5)
```


---

### Modern Direction Lines

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-6">

These are the direction lines (in PCA and map form) for modern-day ellipses. The axis of greatest variation is (primarily) east-west. Ellipses tend to elongate along the coast, demonstrating that the elongation of these ellipses retains important information about the positioning of species ranges.

Keep in mind that, for both of these plots, ellipse elongation is determined by the *degree* of oblongness, and all vectors are scaled identically. The sizes of ellipses are not used. Therefore, smaller vectors represent ellipses that are rounder in shape, while longer vectors represent ellipses that are very oblong.

```{r modern_ellipse_plot, echo=TRUE}
# PCA
pca_data <- ellipse_data[c("r","s")]
pca <- princomp(pca_data)
prop_var <- c(pca$sdev[1]^2/(pca$sdev[1]^2+pca$sdev[2]^2),pca$sdev[2]^2/(pca$sdev[1]^2+pca$sdev[2]^2))

# PCA Plot
plot_pca <- ggplot(ellipse_data) +
  lims(x=c(-30,30),y=c(-30,30)) +
  labs(x="West ------- East", y="South ------- North") +
  geom_segment(x=0,y=0,xend=ellipse_data$r,yend=ellipse_data$s,alpha=0.5,color=lines_color) +
  geom_segment(x=0,y=0,xend=-1*ellipse_data$r,yend=-1*ellipse_data$s,alpha=0.5,color=lines_color) +
  geom_segment(x=0,y=0,xend=pca$loadings[1,2]*prop_var[2]*20,yend=pca$loadings[2,2]*prop_var[2]*20,col=color_3,arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm")),lwd=1.25) +
  geom_segment(x=0,y=0,xend=-1*pca$loadings[1,2]*prop_var[2]*20,yend=-1*pca$loadings[2,2]*prop_var[2]*20,col=color_3,arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm")),lwd=1.25) +
  geom_segment(x=0,y=0,xend=pca$loadings[1,1]*prop_var[1]*20,yend=pca$loadings[2,1]*prop_var[1]*20,col=color_1,arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm")),lwd=1.25) +
  geom_segment(x=0,y=0,xend=-1*pca$loadings[1,1]*prop_var[1]*20,yend=-1*pca$loadings[2,1]*prop_var[1]*20,col=color_1,arrow=arrow(length=unit(0.2,"cm")),lwd=1.25) +
  annotate("text",x=30,y=30,hjust=1,label=paste0("PC1: ",round(prop_var[1],3)*100,"%, PC2: ",round(prop_var[2],3)*100,"%"),color=lines_color) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text = element_blank(),
        axis.ticks = element_blank(),
        axis.title = element_text(color=lines_color),
        panel.grid = element_blank(),
        panel.border = element_rect(color=lines_color,linewidth=2),
        plot.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color),
        axis.title.x = element_text(size=16),
        axis.title.y = element_text(size=16),
        aspect.ratio = 1)
ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"elongation_pca.png"),plot_pca,dpi=600)

# Vector Plot
plot_with_vectors <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
  geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
  geom_segment(data=ellipse_data,x=ellipse_data$x,y=ellipse_data$y,xend=ellipse_data$x+ellipse_data$r*.05,yend=ellipse_data$y+ellipse_data$s*.05,color=ellipse_color,lwd=.75) +
  geom_segment(data=ellipse_data,x=ellipse_data$x,y=ellipse_data$y,xend=ellipse_data$x-ellipse_data$r*.05,yend=ellipse_data$y-ellipse_data$s*.05,color=ellipse_color,lwd=.75) +
  theme_void() +
  theme(panel.border = element_rect(color=lines_color,linewidth=2),
        panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color))
ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"australia_with_vectors.png"),plot_with_vectors,dpi=600)

# Combining
vectors_and_map <- plot_pca + plot_with_vectors +
  plot_annotation(theme = theme(plot.background = element_rect(color=background_color,fill=background_color,linewidth=2)))
ggsave(paste0(range_plot_directory,"vectors_and_map.png"),vectors_and_map,dpi=600,width=7,height=3.5)
```

### Aridity

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-8">

Here are precipitation rasters for 3 different times: 40 Ma, 20 Ma, and present. The two older rasters are obtained from Pohl et al. 2022, and are transformed using the `rgplates` package under the PALEOMAP model. The Pohl et al. 2022 reconstructions overestimate precipitation at the present, and the areas of greatest aridity are not precisely located, so we instead use modern data collected by the Australian Government Bureau of Meteorology over 30 years (1991 to 2020). All rasters are cropped to only include cells that are within Australia and that are present for all time slices. Additionally, we interpolate between these time slices via per-cell linear models to create rasters coinciding with node timings.

</div>
<div class = "col-md-4">

```{r rainfall_maps_show, eval=TRUE, results=TRUE}
include_graphics(paste0(handmade_plot_directory,"rainfall.png"))
```

</div>
</div>

```{r process_rasters, echo=TRUE}
# GPLATES RECONSTRUCTIONS -- DO NOT RERUN
if (RERUN) {
  bbox <- st_bbox(australia_shape)
  
  raster_present_csv <- read.csv(paste0(aridity_data,"pohl/0Ma_Pohletal2022_DIB_PhaneroContinentalClimate.csv"))
  raster_present_data <- data.frame(raster_present_csv[,c(2,1,7)])
  colnames(raster_present_data) <- c("longitude", "latitude", "precip")
  coordinates(raster_present_data) <- ~ longitude + latitude
  gridded(raster_present_data) <- TRUE
  raster_present <- raster(raster_present_data)
  raster_present_australia <- crop(raster_present,bbox)
  raster_present_australia <- raster::mask(raster_present_australia,as(australia_shape,"Spatial"))
  raster_present_points_australia <- as(raster_present_australia,"SpatialPixelsDataFrame")
  raster_present_australia_data <- as.data.frame(raster_present_points_australia)
  
  raster_20_csv <- read.csv(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"pohl/20Ma_Pohletal2022_DIB_PhaneroContinentalClimate.csv"))
  raster_20_data <- data.frame(raster_20_csv[,c(2,1,7)])
  colnames(raster_20_data) <- c("longitude", "latitude", "precip")
  
  raster_20_data <- raster_20_data[which(raster_20_data$longitude>(bbox$xmin-20)),]
  raster_20_data <- raster_20_data[which(raster_20_data$longitude<(bbox$xmax+20)),]
  raster_20_data <- raster_20_data[which(raster_20_data$latitude>(bbox$ymin-20)),]
  raster_20_data <- raster_20_data[which(raster_20_data$latitude<(bbox$ymax+20)),]
  raster_20_coords <- as.matrix(raster_20_data[,c(1,2)])
  reconstruction <- reconstruct(raster_20_coords, from=20, model="PALEOMAP")
  
  raster_20_data$longitude <- reconstruction[,1]
  raster_20_data$latitude <- reconstruction[,2]
  raster_20_data <- na.omit(raster_20_data)
  coordinates(raster_20_data) <- ~ longitude + latitude
  
  raster_20 <- rasterize(raster_20_data,raster_present)
  raster_20_australia <- crop(raster_20,bbox)
  raster_20_points_australia <- as(raster_20_australia,"SpatialPixelsDataFrame")
  raster_20_australia_data <- as.data.frame(raster_20_points_australia)
  
  raster_40_csv <- read.csv(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"pohl/40Ma_Pohletal2022_DIB_PhaneroContinentalClimate.csv"))
  raster_40_data <- data.frame(raster_40_csv[,c(2,1,7)])
  colnames(raster_40_data) <- c("longitude", "latitude", "precip")
  
  raster_40_data <- raster_40_data[which(raster_40_data$longitude>(bbox$xmin-20)),]
  raster_40_data <- raster_40_data[which(raster_40_data$longitude<(bbox$xmax+20)),]
  raster_40_data <- raster_40_data[which(raster_40_data$latitude>(bbox$ymin-20)),]
  raster_40_data <- raster_40_data[which(raster_40_data$latitude<(bbox$ymax+20)),]
  raster_40_coords <- as.matrix(raster_40_data[,c(1,2)])
  reconstruction <- reconstruct(raster_40_coords, from=40, model="PALEOMAP")
  
  raster_40_data$longitude <- reconstruction[,1]
  raster_40_data$latitude <- reconstruction[,2]
  raster_40_data <- na.omit(raster_40_data)
  coordinates(raster_40_data) <- ~ longitude + latitude
  
  raster_40 <- rasterize(raster_40_data,raster_present)
  raster_40_australia <- crop(raster_40,bbox)
  raster_40_points_australia <- as(raster_40_australia,"SpatialPixelsDataFrame")
  raster_40_australia_data <- as.data.frame(raster_40_points_australia)
  
  raster_bom_file <- raster(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"bom/rainan.txt"))
  raster_bom_file_scaled <- raster_bom_file / 365.25
  raster_bom_data <- data.frame(rasterToPoints(raster_bom_file_scaled))
  colnames(raster_bom_data) <- c("longitude", "latitude", "precip")
  
  raster_bom_data <- raster_bom_data[which(raster_bom_data$longitude>(bbox$xmin-20)),]
  raster_bom_data <- raster_bom_data[which(raster_bom_data$longitude<(bbox$xmax+20)),]
  raster_bom_data <- raster_bom_data[which(raster_bom_data$latitude>(bbox$ymin-20)),]
  raster_bom_data <- raster_bom_data[which(raster_bom_data$latitude<(bbox$ymax+20)),]
  raster_bom_coords <- as.matrix(raster_bom_data[,c(1,2)])
  raster_bom_data <- na.omit(raster_bom_data)
  coordinates(raster_bom_data) <- ~ longitude + latitude
  
  raster_bom <- rasterize(raster_bom_data,raster_present)
  raster_bom_australia <- crop(raster_bom,bbox)
  raster_bom_points_australia <- as(raster_bom_australia,"SpatialPixelsDataFrame")
  raster_bom_australia_data <- as.data.frame(raster_bom_points_australia)
  
  in_present <- paste(raster_present_australia_data$x,raster_present_australia_data$y)
  in_20 <- paste(raster_20_australia_data$x,raster_20_australia_data$y)
  in_40 <- paste(raster_40_australia_data$x,raster_40_australia_data$y)
  in_bom <- paste(raster_bom_australia_data$x,raster_bom_australia_data$y)
  
  pairs_present <- c()
  for (i in 1:nrow(raster_present_australia_data)) {
    keep <- TRUE
    if (!(in_present[i] %in% in_20)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_present[i] %in% in_40)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_present[i] %in% in_bom)) {keep <- FALSE}
    pairs_present <- c(pairs_present,keep)
  }
  
  pairs_20 <- c()
  for (i in 1:nrow(raster_20_australia_data)) {
    keep <- TRUE
    if (!(in_20[i] %in% in_present)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_20[i] %in% in_40)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_20[i] %in% in_bom)) {keep <- FALSE}
    pairs_20 <- c(pairs_20,keep)
  }
  
  pairs_40 <- c()
  for (i in 1:nrow(raster_40_australia_data)) {
    keep <- TRUE
    if (!(in_40[i] %in% in_present)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_40[i] %in% in_20)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_40[i] %in% in_bom)) {keep <- FALSE}
    pairs_40 <- c(pairs_40,keep)
  }
  
  pairs_bom <- c()
  for (i in 1:nrow(raster_bom_australia_data)) {
    keep <- TRUE
    if (!(in_bom[i] %in% in_present)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_bom[i] %in% in_20)) {keep <- FALSE}
    if (!(in_bom[i] %in% in_40)) {keep <- FALSE}
    pairs_bom <- c(pairs_bom,keep)
  }
  
  raster_present_australia_data <- raster_present_australia_data[pairs_present,]
  raster_20_australia_data <- raster_20_australia_data[pairs_20,]
  raster_40_australia_data <- raster_40_australia_data[pairs_40,]
  raster_bom_australia_data <- raster_bom_australia_data[pairs_bom,]
  
  write.csv(raster_present_australia_data,paste0(aridity_data_directory,"Ma0_raster.csv"),row.names=FALSE)
  write.csv(raster_20_australia_data,paste0(aridity_data_directory,"Ma20_raster.csv"),row.names=FALSE)
  write.csv(raster_40_australia_data,paste0(aridity_data_directory,"Ma40_raster.csv"),row.names=FALSE)
  write.csv(raster_bom_australia_data,paste0(aridity_data_directory,"bom_raster.csv"),row.names=FALSE)
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

```{r interpolate_rasters}
raster_bom_australia_data <- data.frame(read.csv(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"bom_raster.csv")))
raster_20_australia_data <- data.frame(read.csv(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"Ma20_raster.csv")))
raster_40_australia_data <- data.frame(read.csv(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"Ma40_raster.csv")))

branching_times <- branching.times(tree)
sorted_branching_times <- sort(branching_times)
node_indices <- names(sorted_branching_times)
node_times <- unname(unlist(sorted_branching_times))
times_young <- data.frame(x=node_times[node_times < 20])
times_old <- data.frame(x=node_times[node_times > 20])

raster_data <- data.frame(cbind(raster_bom_australia_data$x,raster_bom_australia_data$y,raster_bom_australia_data$precip,raster_20_australia_data$precip,raster_40_australia_data$precip))
colnames(raster_data) <- c("x","y","Ma0","Ma20","Ma40")
for (i in 1:length(node_indices)) {
  raster_data[[node_indices[i]]] <- NA
}

models_first <- list()
models_second <- list()
for (i in 1:nrow(raster_data)) {
  x_first <- c(0,20)
  x_second <- c(20,40)
  y_first <- c(raster_data$Ma0[i],raster_data$Ma20[i])
  y_second <- c(raster_data$Ma20[i],raster_data$Ma40[i])
  first <- data.frame(x=x_first,y=y_first)
  second <- data.frame(x=x_second,y=y_second)
  models_first[[i]] <- lm(y~x,data=first)
  models_second[[i]] <- lm(y~x,data=second)
  young_preds <- unname(unlist(predict(models_first[[i]],times_young)))
  old_preds <- unname(unlist(predict(models_second[[i]],times_old)))
  preds <- c(young_preds,old_preds)
  raster_data[i,6:(5+length(node_indices))] <- preds
}

models_first_deltas <- c()
for (i in 1:length(models_first)) {
  slope <- coef(models_first[[i]])[2]
  models_first_deltas <- c(models_first_deltas,slope)
}
models_second_deltas <- c()
for (i in 1:length(models_second)) {
  slope <- coef(models_second[[i]])[2]
  models_second_deltas <- c(models_second_deltas,slope)
}

raster_data$delta1 <- -1*models_first_deltas
raster_data$delta2 <- -1*models_second_deltas

write.csv(raster_data,paste0(aridity_data_directory,"interpolated_raster.csv"),row.names=FALSE)
```

```{r read_rasters, eval=TRUE}
raster_data <- data.frame(read.csv(paste0(aridity_data_directory,"interpolated_raster.csv")))
```

```{r aridity_maps, echo=TRUE}
plot_raster <- function(raster_data,column,name) {
  plot <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
    geom_tile(data=raster_data,aes_string(x="x",y="y",fill=column)) +
    scale_fill_continuous(limits=c(0,12.5),type="viridis") +
    labs(fill="Precipitation \n(mm / day)") +
    geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=name,color=lines_color,size=12,x=min(raster_data$x),y=max(raster_data$y),hjust=.1,vjust=.3) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
          legend.position=c(.985,.98),
          legend.justification = c("right","top"),
          legend.title = element_text(color=lines_color,size=16),
          legend.text = element_text(color=lines_color,size=16),
          legend.box.background = element_rect(fill=background_color, color=lines_color),
          legend.margin = margin(1,1,1,1),
          legend.spacing.y = unit(.5,"cm"),
          legend.key.width = unit(1.5,'cm'),
          legend.key.height = unit(1.5,'cm'))
return(plot)
}

plot_deltas <- function(raster_data,column) {
  plot <- ggplot(australia_shape) +
    geom_tile(data=raster_data,aes_string(x="x",y="y",fill=column)) +
    scale_fill_gradientn(colors=c("red","white","blue"),limits=c(-.4,.4),values=c(0,.5,1)) +
    labs(fill="Change / MY \n(mm / day)") +
    geom_sf(fill=NA,color=lines_color) +
    theme_void() +
    theme(panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
          legend.position=c(.985,.98),
          legend.justification = c("right","top"),
          legend.title = element_text(color=lines_color,size=16),
          legend.text = element_text(color=lines_color,size=16),
          legend.box.background = element_rect(fill=background_color, color=lines_color),
          legend.margin = margin(1,1,1,1),
          legend.spacing.y = unit(.5,"cm"),
          legend.key.width = unit(1.5,'cm'),
          legend.key.height = unit(1.5,'cm'))
return(plot)
}

present_plot <- plot_raster(raster_data,"Ma0","0 Ma")
Ma20_plot <- plot_raster(raster_data,"Ma20","20 Ma")
Ma40_plot <- plot_raster(raster_data,"Ma40","40 Ma")
delta1_plot <- plot_deltas(raster_data,"delta1")
delta2_plot <- plot_deltas(raster_data,"delta2")

layout <- "
AABBCC
AABBCC
#DDEE#
#DDEE#
"

aridity_plot <- Ma40_plot + Ma20_plot + present_plot + delta2_plot + delta1_plot + plot_layout(design = layout, guides = "collect") & theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA))

ggsave(paste0(aridity_plot_directory,"aridity_maps.png"),aridity_plot,dpi=600,width=15,height=8)
```

---

### Phylogenetic Tree

Here is the tree being used, including node numbers. Three major monophyletic clades are identified: *Ctenotus*, *Lerista*, and "Third Clade". The "Third Clade" includes all other Australian sphenomorphine skinks, except *Notoscincus ornatus*, which is sister to the clade containing both *Ctenotus* and *Lerista*. I will try to be consistent with coloring throughout the figures: *Ctenotus* will be shown in orange, *Lerista* in green, and the "Third Clade" in purple. Node and tip numbering is consistent with the EMPIRE analyses and ellipse data (but NOT the shapefile containing polygon ranges).

```{r plot_tree, echo=TRUE}
tree_2 <- groupClade(tree,c(222,310,390))
labels <- tree_2$tip.label
numbers <- c(1:218)
for (i in 1:length(labels)) {
  labels[i] <- paste0(labels[i]," [",numbers[i],"]")
}
tree_2$tip.label <- labels
tree_plot <- ggtree(tree_2,aes(color=group),layout="dendrogram") +
  scale_color_manual(name="Clade",values=c(color_1,color_2,color_3,color_4)) +
  guides(color="none") +
  geom_cladelab(node=222,label="Ctenotus, 89 sp.",fontsize=18,barsize=1,textcolor=color_2,barcolor=color_2,offset=9,vjust=1.5,hjust=.5) +
  geom_cladelab(node=310,label="Lerista, 81 sp.",fontsize=18,barsize=1,textcolor=color_3,barcolor=color_3,offset=9,vjust=1.5,hjust=.5) +
  geom_cladelab(node=390,label="Third Clade, 47 sp.",fontsize=18,barsize=1,textcolor=color_4,barcolor=color_4,offset=9,vjust=1.5,hjust=.5) +
  geom_tiplab() +
  geom_nodelab(geom="label",aes(label=node),fill=background_color,angle=0,vjust=0) +
  ggplot2::xlim(11,-38) +
  theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
        panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA))
ggsave(paste0(tree_plot_directory,"tree.png"),tree_plot,dpi=600,height=16,width=40)
```

```{r plot_tree_2}
tree_2 <- groupClade(tree,c(222,310,390))

node_categories[310] <- color_3
node_categories[390] <- color_4

node_sizes <- node_categories
for (i in 1:length(node_categories)) {
  if (node_categories[i] == lines_color) {node_sizes[i] <- .5}
  if (node_categories[i] == color_2) {node_sizes[i] <- 16}
  if (node_categories[i] == color_3) {node_sizes[i] <- 16}
  if (node_categories[i] == color_4) {node_sizes[i] <- 16}
}

node_sizes <- as.numeric(node_sizes)

tree_plot <- ggtree(tree_2,aes(color=group),layout="dendrogram") +
  geom_tree(linewidth=2) +
  scale_color_manual(name="Clade",values=c(lines_color,lines_color,lines_color,lines_color)) +
  guides(color="none") +
  geom_nodepoint(fill=node_categories,size=node_sizes,pch=21,stroke=0) +
  geom_cladelab(node=222,label="Ctenotus, 89 sp.",fontsize=18,barsize=2,textcolor=color_2,barcolor=color_2,offset=1,vjust=1.5,hjust=.5) +
  geom_cladelab(node=310,label="Lerista, 81 sp.",fontsize=18,barsize=2,textcolor=color_3,barcolor=color_3,offset=1,vjust=1.5,hjust=.5) +
  geom_cladelab(node=390,label="Third Clade, 47 sp.",fontsize=18,barsize=2,textcolor=color_4,barcolor=color_4,offset=1,vjust=1.5,hjust=.5) +
  ggplot2::xlim(3,-38) +
  theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
        panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA))
ggsave(paste0(tree_plot_directory,"tree_2.png"),tree_plot,dpi=600,height=16,width=40)
```

<!--
###########################################################
###########################################################
SIMULATIONS: COVERAGE, MISSING TAXA, NOISE, TREE SIZE, RASE
###########################################################
###########################################################
-->

## Simulations

### Coverage

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-5">

These are the results of the completed coverage experiment. Coverages look good across all parameters. Estimates for rates are very good. For OU parameters, estimates for $\mu$ are excellent, and estimates for $\kappa$ are good. Estimates for root values of $r$ and $s$ are good. Estimates for root values of $x$ and $y$ are fairly diffuse. Estimates for root values of $a$ are only good when $a$ has a reasonably large value at the root (to be expected).

```{r coverage, echo=TRUE}
# SIMULATION COVERAGE -- DO NOT RERUN WITHOUT NEW SIMULATIONS
if (RERUN) {
  burnin <- 20000
  completed_rows <- 200000
  params_list <- c("sigma_x","sigma_y","sigma_r","sigma_s","sigma_a","mu","kappa","root_x","root_y","root_r","root_s","root_a")
  simulation_numbers <- sprintf("%03.0f", 1:200)
  nsims <- length(simulation_numbers)
  sim_data <- data.frame(matrix(ncol=9,nrow=0))
  row <- 1
  for (num in simulation_numbers) {
    cat("\r","Simulation ", num, " | ", round(as.numeric(num)/nsims*100,0), "%")
    directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,num,"/")
    iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
    if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
    if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {
      treefile <- ape::read.tree(paste0(directory,"true.tree.txt"))
      tree_size <- length(treefile$tip.label)
      logfile <- read.csv(paste0(directory,"model_log.tsv"),sep="\t",skipNul=TRUE)
      truefile <- strsplit(readLines(paste0(directory,"true.param.tsv")),"\t")
      true_vals <- c()
      for (i in 1:length(truefile)) {
        true_vals$param[i] <- truefile[[i]][1]
        true_vals$vals[i] <- paste(truefile[[i]][-1],collapse=",")
      }
      mcmc <- logfile[-c(1:burnin),]
      for (param in params_list) {
        param_true <- as.numeric(true_vals$vals[which(true_vals$param==param)])
        param_mcmc <- mcmc[[param]]
        param_est <- mean(param_mcmc)
        param_hpd <- hdi(param_mcmc)
        param_hpd_low <- param_hpd[[1]]
        param_hpd_high <- param_hpd[[2]]
        if (param_hpd_low <= param_true & param_true <= param_hpd_high) {param_covered <- TRUE} else {param_covered <- FALSE}
        param_ess <- ess(param_mcmc)
        param_row <- c(num,param,param_true,param_est,param_hpd_low,param_hpd_high,param_covered,param_ess,tree_size)
        sim_data <- rbind(sim_data,param_row)
        row <- row + 1
      }
    }
  }
  colnames(sim_data) <- c("sim","param","true","est","hpd_low","hpd_high","covered","ess","tree_size")
  write.table(sim_data,file=paste0(simulation_output_directory,"sim_results.tsv"),quote=FALSE,sep="\t",row.names=FALSE)
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

``` {r coverage_plots, echo=TRUE} 
sim_data <- read.csv(paste0(simulation_output_directory,"sim_results.tsv"),sep="\t")
sim_data <- sim_data[which(sim_data$ess >= 200),]

plot_param <- function(param, name) {
  data_subset <- sim_data[which(sim_data$param == param),]
  coverage <- round(sum(data_subset$covered==TRUE)/length(data_subset$covered) * 100)
  min <- min(min(data_subset$hpd_low),min(data_subset$true),min(data_subset$est))
  max <- max(max(data_subset$hpd_high),max(data_subset$true),max(data_subset$est))
  plot <- ggplot(data_subset, aes(x=true, y=est)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=hpd_low,ymax=hpd_high,col=covered),linewidth=.25) +
    scale_color_manual(values=c(color_2,color_1)) +
    geom_point(size=.5,pch=16,color=lines_color) +
    geom_abline(slope=1,linewidth=.25,color=lines_color) +
    labs(x=NULL,y=NULL) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=name,x=min,y=max,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=paste(coverage,"%",sep=""),x=max,y=min,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    lims(x=c(min,max),y=c(min,max)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color=lines_color,linewidth=2),
          panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
          axis.text = element_text(color=lines_color),
          axis.ticks = element_line(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=1,
          panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major=element_blank(),
          legend.position="none",
          plot.margin=margin(5,5,5,5,"pt"))
  return(plot)
}

plot_sigma_x <- plot_param("sigma_x",bquote(sigma[x]*.("*")))
plot_sigma_y <- plot_param("sigma_y",bquote(sigma[y]*.("*")))
plot_sigma_r <- plot_param("sigma_r",bquote(sigma[r]))

          panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),

          axis.title=element_text(hjust=.5,size=14,color=lines_color),
```

``` {r coverage_plots_manuscript} 
}
```

---

### Sensitivity to Missing Taxa

These simulations represent a scenario where 50% of species either go extinct before the present, or are not sampled for other reasons. Root values are not recovered especially well with or without extinction, although extinction does lead to a few extreme outliers which are not seen when the model is correctly specified (for example, inferring root values for $x$ and $y$ on the order of 10,000). It is not clear why this happens.

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-5">

As expected, rates of evolution of $x$ and $y$ are overestimated, because we fail to observe some cladogenetic events where $x$ and $y$ would change. Additionally, the rate of evolution of $a$ is overestimated. This is likely for the same reason, although the situation is a bit more complex. Generally, cladogenetic scenarios make $a$ smaller, which is counteracted by $\mu$ and $\kappa$ pulling $a$ toward larger values. If we don't observe some cladogenetic events, we might assume that $a$ stays small, and has more time to evolve toward the optimum (which would cause an underestimation of $\kappa$). However, we do not see this; the deterministic part of the OU process seems robust to missing cladogenetic events. Instead, we observe an overestimate of $\sigma_a$. It is also interesting that we overestimate rates of evolution for $r$ and $s$, parameters which do not change at cladogenesis. The model is likely attempting to compensate for shifts in $x$ and $y$ by expanding the influence of the cladogenetic events which are observed, necessitating more elongated ellipses at those times.

```{r sensitivity, echo=TRUE}
# SIMULATION SENSITIVITY -- DO NOT RERUN WITHOUT NEW SIMULATIONS
if (RERUN) {
  burnin <- 20000
  completed_rows <- 200000
  params_list <- c("sigma_x","sigma_y","sigma_r","sigma_s","sigma_a","mu","kappa","root_x","root_y","root_r","root_s","root_a")
  simulation_numbers <- sprintf("%03.0f", 1:100)
  nsims <- length(simulation_numbers)
  sim_data <- data.frame(matrix(ncol=7,nrow=0))
  row <- 1
  for (num in simulation_numbers) {
    cat("\r","Simulation ", num, " | ", round(as.numeric(num)/nsims*100,0), "%")
    directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,num,"/")
    if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"sensitivity/model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"sensitivity/model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"sensitivity/model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
    } else {iterations <- 0}
    if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {
      logfile <- read.csv(paste0(directory,"sensitivity/model_log.tsv"),sep="\t",skipNul=TRUE)
      truefile <- strsplit(readLines(paste0(directory,"true.param.tsv")),"\t")
      true_vals <- c()
      for (i in 1:length(truefile)) {
        true_vals$param[i] <- truefile[[i]][1]
        true_vals$vals[i] <- paste(truefile[[i]][-1],collapse=",")
      }
      mcmc <- logfile[-c(1:burnin),]
      for (param in params_list) {
        param_true <- as.numeric(true_vals$vals[which(true_vals$param==param)])
        param_mcmc <- mcmc[[param]]
        param_est <- mean(param_mcmc)
        param_hpd <- hdi(param_mcmc)
        param_hpd_low <- param_hpd[[1]]
        param_hpd_high <- param_hpd[[2]]
        param_ess <- ess(param_mcmc)
        param_row <- c(num,param,param_true,param_est,param_hpd_low,param_hpd_high,param_ess)
        sim_data <- rbind(sim_data,param_row)
        row <- row + 1
      }
    }
  }
  colnames(sim_data) <- c("sim","param","true","est","hpd_low","hpd_high","ess")
  write.table(sim_data,file=paste0(simulation_output_directory,"sensitivity_results.tsv"),quote=FALSE,sep="\t",row.names=FALSE)
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

``` {r sensitivity_plots, echo=TRUE} 
sim_data <- read.csv(paste0(simulation_output_directory,"sensitivity_results.tsv"),sep="\t")
sim_data <- sim_data[which(sim_data$ess >= 200),]

plot_param <- function(param, name) {
  data_subset <- sim_data[which(sim_data$param == param),]
  high_vals <- sort(data_subset$hpd_high)
  low_vals <- sort(data_subset$hpd_low)
  range <- c(low_vals[3],high_vals[length(high_vals)-2])
  high_limit <- high_vals[length(high_vals)-2] + .5 * (range[2] - range[1])
  low_limit <- low_vals[3] - .5 * (range[2] - range[1])
  keep <- c()
  for (i in 1:nrow(data_subset)) {
    keep[i] <- TRUE
    if (data_subset$hpd_high[i] > high_limit) {
      keep[i] <- FALSE
    }
    if (data_subset$hpd_low[i] < low_limit) {
      keep[i] <- FALSE
    }
  }
  n_outliers <- sum(keep==FALSE)
  data_subset <- data_subset[keep==TRUE,]
  min <- min(min(data_subset$hpd_low),min(data_subset$true),min(data_subset$est))
  max <- max(max(data_subset$hpd_high),max(data_subset$true),max(data_subset$est))
  plot <- ggplot(data_subset, aes(x=true, y=est)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=hpd_low,ymax=hpd_high),linewidth=.25,color=color_1) +
    geom_point(size=.5,pch=16,color=lines_color) +
    geom_abline(slope=1,linewidth=.25,color=lines_color) +
    labs(x=NULL,y=NULL) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=name,x=min,y=max,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=paste("Outliers: ",n_outliers),x=max,y=min,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    lims(x=c(min,max),y=c(min,max)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color=lines_color,linewidth=2),
          panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
          axis.text = element_text(color=lines_color),
          axis.ticks = element_line(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=1,
          panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major=element_blank(),
          legend.position="none",
          plot.margin=margin(5,5,5,5,"pt"))
  return(plot)
}

axis_plot <- ggplot() +
    labs(x="True (Simulated) Parameter Value",y="Posterior Mean and 95% HPD Interval") +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),
          panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),
          line=element_blank(),
          axis.title=element_text(hjust=.5,size=14,color=lines_color),
          axis.text=element_text(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=5/7)
inner_plot <- plot_sigma_x + plot_root_x + plot_sigma_y + plot_root_y + plot_sigma_r + plot_root_r + plot_sigma_s + plot_root_s + plot_sigma_a + plot_root_a + plot_mu + plot_kappa + plot_layout(ncol = 4) & theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color))

ggsave(paste0(simulation_plot_directory,"sensitivity.png"),sensitivity_plot,dpi=600,width=7,height=5)
```

---

### Sensitivity to Noise

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-5">

These simulations represent a scenario where the ellipses at the tips have some amount of noise, possibly due to sampling issues. For each simulated dataset, we generate "noise" based on the range of observed values at the tips for a particular parameter ($max - min$). The extra noise for each parameter is $\sim Uniform(-0.1 \times range, 0.1 \times range)$. This means that noisy ellipses may differ substantially from the true ellipses (up to 10% of the variation in the entire dataset). As expected, additional variation due to noise causes rates to be generally overestimated. (Note: 38 has some rounding because it had to be copied from the joblog).

```{r noisy, echo=TRUE}
# SIMULATION NOISE -- DO NOT RERUN WITHOUT NEW SIMULATIONS
if (RERUN) {
  burnin <- 20000
  completed_rows <- 200000
  params_list <- c("sigma_x","sigma_y","sigma_r","sigma_s","sigma_a","mu","kappa","root_x","root_y","root_r","root_s","root_a")
  simulation_numbers <- sprintf("%03.0f", 1:100)
  nsims <- length(simulation_numbers)
  sim_data <- data.frame(matrix(ncol=7,nrow=0))
  row <- 1
  for (num in simulation_numbers) {
    cat("\r","Simulation ", num, " | ", round(as.numeric(num)/nsims*100,0), "%")
    directory <- paste0(sim_prefix,num,"/")
    if (file.exists(paste0(directory,"noisy/model_log.tsv"))) {
        iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 1 ",directory,"noisy/model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])
        if (is.na(iterations)) {iterations <- as.numeric(strsplit(system(paste0("tail -n 2 ",directory,"noisy/model_log.tsv"),intern=TRUE),"\t")[[1]][1])}
    } else {iterations <- 0}
    if (iterations >= completed_rows * 10) {
      logfile <- read.csv(paste0(directory,"noisy/model_log.tsv"),sep="\t",skipNul=TRUE)
      truefile <- strsplit(readLines(paste0(directory,"true.param.tsv")),"\t")
      true_vals <- c()
      for (i in 1:length(truefile)) {
        true_vals$param[i] <- truefile[[i]][1]
        true_vals$vals[i] <- paste(truefile[[i]][-1],collapse=",")
      }
      mcmc <- logfile[-c(1:burnin),]
      for (param in params_list) {
        param_true <- as.numeric(true_vals$vals[which(true_vals$param==param)])
        param_mcmc <- mcmc[[param]]
        param_est <- mean(param_mcmc)
        param_hpd <- hdi(param_mcmc)
        param_hpd_low <- param_hpd[[1]]
        param_hpd_high <- param_hpd[[2]]
        param_ess <- ess(param_mcmc)
        param_row <- c(num,param,param_true,param_est,param_hpd_low,param_hpd_high,param_ess)
        sim_data <- rbind(sim_data,param_row)
        row <- row + 1
      }
    }
  }
  colnames(sim_data) <- c("sim","param","true","est","hpd_low","hpd_high","ess")
  write.table(sim_data,file=paste0(simulation_output_directory,"noisy_results.tsv"),quote=FALSE,sep="\t",row.names=FALSE)
  RERUN <- FALSE
}
```

``` {r noisy_plots, echo=TRUE} 
sim_data <- read.csv(paste0(simulation_output_directory,"noisy_results.tsv"),sep="\t")
sim_data <- sim_data[which(sim_data$ess >= 200),]

plot_param <- function(param, name) {
  data_subset <- sim_data[which(sim_data$param == param),]
  high_vals <- sort(data_subset$hpd_high)
  low_vals <- sort(data_subset$hpd_low)
  range <- c(low_vals[3],high_vals[length(high_vals)-2])
  high_limit <- high_vals[length(high_vals)-2] + .5 * (range[2] - range[1])
  low_limit <- low_vals[3] - .5 * (range[2] - range[1])
  keep <- c()
  for (i in 1:nrow(data_subset)) {
    keep[i] <- TRUE
    if (data_subset$hpd_high[i] > high_limit) {
      keep[i] <- FALSE
    }
    if (data_subset$hpd_low[i] < low_limit) {
      keep[i] <- FALSE
    }
  }
  n_outliers <- sum(keep==FALSE)
  data_subset <- data_subset[keep==TRUE,]
  min <- min(min(data_subset$hpd_low),min(data_subset$true),min(data_subset$est))
  max <- max(max(data_subset$hpd_high),max(data_subset$true),max(data_subset$est))
  plot <- ggplot(data_subset, aes(x=true, y=est)) +
    geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=hpd_low,ymax=hpd_high),linewidth=.25,color=color_1) +
    geom_point(size=.5,pch=16,color=lines_color) +
    geom_abline(slope=1,linewidth=.25,color=lines_color) +
    labs(x=NULL,y=NULL) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=name,x=min,y=max,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=paste("Outliers: ",n_outliers),x=max,y=min,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    lims(x=c(min,max),y=c(min,max)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color=lines_color,linewidth=2),
          panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
          axis.text = element_text(color=lines_color),
          axis.ticks = element_line(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=1,
          panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major=element_blank(),
          legend.position="none",
          plot.margin=margin(5,5,5,5,"pt"))
  return(plot)
}

          panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),
          line=element_blank(),
          axis.title=element_text(hjust=.5,size=14,color=lines_color),
          axis.text=element_text(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=5/7)
inner_plot <- plot_sigma_x + plot_root_x + plot_sigma_y + plot_root_y + plot_sigma_r + plot_root_r + plot_sigma_s + plot_root_s + plot_sigma_a + plot_root_a + plot_mu + plot_kappa + plot_layout(ncol = 4) & theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color))
noisy_plot <- axis_plot + inset_element(inner_plot,left=0,bottom=0,right=1,top=1) & theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color))

ggsave(paste0(simulation_plot_directory,"noisy.png"),noisy_plot,dpi=600,width=7,height=5)
```

### Tree sizes

<div class = "row">
<div class = "col-md-5">

This plot uses the same simulations as the coverage plot, but instead shows the quality of estimates based on tree sizes. Most trees (all between 20 and 250 taxa) behave similarly. With smaller trees, it is somewhat easier to estimate the root, and somewhat more difficult to estimate rate parameters. This is what we would expect.

``` {r treesize_plots, echo=TRUE} 
sim_data <- read.csv(paste0(simulation_output_directory,"sim_results.tsv"),sep="\t")
sim_data <- sim_data[which(sim_data$ess >= 200),]

plot_param <- function(param, name) {
  data_subset <- sim_data[which(sim_data$param == param),]
  min <- min(min(data_subset$hpd_low),min(data_subset$true),min(data_subset$est))
  max <- max(max(data_subset$hpd_high),max(data_subset$true),max(data_subset$est))
  plot <- ggplot(data_subset, aes(x=true, y=est)) +
    geom_point(size=.5,pch=16,aes(color=tree_size)) +
    geom_abline(slope=1,linewidth=.25,color=lines_color) +
    labs(x=NULL,y=NULL) +
    annotate(geom="text",label=name,x=min,y=max,hjust="inward",vjust="inward",color=lines_color) +
    lims(x=c(min,max),y=c(min,max)) +
    theme_bw() +
    theme(panel.border = element_rect(color=lines_color,linewidth=2),
          panel.background = element_rect(fill=background_color,color=NA),
          axis.text = element_text(color=lines_color,size=6),
          axis.ticks = element_line(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=1,
          panel.grid.minor=element_blank(),
          panel.grid.major=element_blank(),
          legend.position="none",
          plot.margin=margin(5,5,5,5,"pt"))
  return(plot)
}

plot_sigma_x <- plot_param("sigma_x",bquote(sigma[x]))
plot_sigma_y <- plot_param("sigma_y",bquote(sigma[y]))
plot_sigma_r <- plot_param("sigma_r",bquote(sigma[r]))
plot_sigma_s <- plot_param("sigma_s",bquote(sigma[s]))
plot_sigma_a <- plot_param("sigma_a",bquote(sigma[a]))
plot_mu <- plot_param("mu",bquote(mu))
plot_kappa <- plot_param("kappa",bquote(kappa))
plot_root_x <- plot_param("root_x",bquote(root[x]))
plot_root_y <- plot_param("root_y",bquote(root[y]))
plot_root_r <- plot_param("root_r",bquote(root[r]))
plot_root_s <- plot_param("root_s",bquote(root[s]))
plot_root_a <- plot_param("root_a",bquote(root[a]))

axis_plot <- ggplot() +
    labs(x="True (Simulated) Parameter Value",y="Posterior Mean and 95% HPD Interval") +
    theme_classic() +
    theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),
          panel.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color),
          line=element_blank(),
          axis.title=element_text(hjust=.5,size=14,color=lines_color),
          axis.text=element_text(color=lines_color),
          aspect.ratio=5/8)
inner_plot <- plot_sigma_x + plot_root_x + plot_sigma_y + plot_root_y + plot_sigma_r + plot_root_r + plot_sigma_s + plot_root_s + plot_sigma_a + plot_root_a + plot_mu + plot_kappa + 
  plot_layout(ncol = 4) + 
  plot_layout(guides="collect") & 
  labs(color="Tree Size") & 
  theme(legend.position="right",
        legend.background=element_rect(color=NA,fill=NA),
        legend.text=element_text(color=lines_color),
        legend.title=element_text(color=lines_color),
        plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color)) & 
  scale_color_gradient(low="yellow",
                       high="blue",
                       aesthetics="color",
                       limits=c(20,250))
treesize_plot <- axis_plot + inset_element(inner_plot,left=0,bottom=0,right=1,top=1) & theme(plot.background=element_rect(fill=background_color,color=background_color))

ggsave(paste0(simulation_plot_directory,"tree_size.png"),treesize_plot,dpi=600,width=7,height=4.5)
```
